summary: Ukrainian forces reported two UAVs shot down near Sevastopol and a drone strike on a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia injuring eight, while Russian attacks hit public transport in Kherson and Kramatorsk, causing civilian casualties. Russian forces claimed to have destroyed a surrounded enemy in Konstantinovka and advanced in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict. Diplomatic talks on a limited ceasefire zone of 50‑70 km along the front line are ongoing.
key_points: Two Ukrainian UAVs were downed near the north side of Sevastopol, according to the regional governor (Source: @tass_agency/381543).
Russian drones struck a logistics operator building in Zaporizhzhia, causing eight injuries, a fire and vehicle damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
In Kherson, Russian forces attacked a public transport route, damaging two buses and wounding five people of moderate severity (Source: @UkraineNow/82046).
Two strikes on Kramatorsk resulted in two deaths and at least six injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82053).
Russian troops reported destroying a surrounded enemy in Konstantinovka and advancing in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict (Source: @tass_agency/381540).
Ukrainian officials discussed a two‑stage ceasefire limiting hostilities to a 50‑70 km zone on both sides of the front line (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31015).
Chemical grenades containing chloropicrin were found in the liberated village of Staromlynivka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31030).
Ukrainian President Zelensky warned Belarus to remove fire‑correction equipment within a week or face Ukrainian action (Source: @UkraineNow/82059).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces suffered UAV losses near Sevastopol while Russian drones hit a logistics hub in Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight. Russian artillery and missile strikes damaged civilian transport in Kherson and caused fatalities in Kramatorsk. Ongoing diplomatic talks consider a limited ceasefire zone of 50‑70 km along the front.

points: Ukrainian authorities reported two UAVs shot down near the north side of Sevastopol, indicating active aerial engagements in the Black Sea region (Source: @tass_agency/381543).
In Zaporizhzhia, a Russian drone strike on a logistics operator building caused eight injuries, including one police officer, ignited a fire and damaged several vehicles (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Ukrainian forces claimed a mass attack on Moscow, as stated by the Moscow mayor, though details were not provided (Source: @tass_agency/381516).

Ukrainian officials disclosed the discovery of 59 chemical grenades containing chloropicrin in the liberated village of Staromlynivka, raising concerns about chemical weapon use (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31030).

Ukrainian troops reportedly used UAVs targeting Russian positions in multiple oblasts, including Sumy (towards Stepanivka), Chernihiv (towards Borzna), and Dnipropetrovsk (towards Pavlohrad and Chaplyne) (Sources: @kpszsu/65605, @kpszsu/65620, @kpszsu/65635).

Russian forces announced the destruction of a surrounded enemy unit in Konstantinovka's southwestern sector and continued offensive operations in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict (Source: @tass_agency/381540).
In Kherson, Russian attacks on a public transport route damaged two buses and resulted in five moderate injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/82046).
Two strikes on Kramatorsk killed two civilians and wounded at least six, highlighting intensified Russian shelling in Donetsk region (Source: @UkraineNow/82053).
Russian statements indicated the liberation of Yurkovka, located 10 km from Sloviansk and Kramatorsk, potentially opening a new axis of advance (Source: @tass_agency/381534).

Multiple UAV sightings were reported heading toward Ukrainian positions from Russian‑held areas, including Komyshuvakha (Zaporizhzhia) and from the south of Zaporizhzhia (Sources: @kpszsu/65631, @kpszsu/65606).

Ukrainian UAV activity was reported in eastern Dnipropetrovsk oblast, with drones heading toward Pavlohrad, Chaplyne, and Petrykivka, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions (Sources: @kpszsu/65635, @kpszsu/65582).
In Sumy oblast, reactive UAVs were observed moving from the south toward Okhtyrka, indicating possible cross‑border incursions (Source: @kpszsu/65609).
UAVs from the north were reported heading toward Stepanivka in Sumy, highlighting contested airspace over the eastern sector (Source: @kpszsu/65605).
Ukrainian forces discussed a ceasefire zone of 50‑70 km on both sides of the front, which would directly affect eastern front operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31015).
Russian advances in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict suggest a push eastward from the Konstantinovka area (Source: @tass_agency/381540).
Ukrainian UAVs were reported over Trubchevsk in Bryansk region, resulting in injury to an 11‑year‑old child, indicating cross‑border drone activity from the north (Source: @tass_agency/381512).
Two Ukrainian UAVs were downed near the north side of Sevastopol, reflecting heightened aerial contest in the northern Black Sea corridor (Source: @tass_agency/381543).

Russian forces claimed to have destroyed a surrounded enemy in Konstantinovka, a location north of the main front line, suggesting ongoing engagements in the northern sector (Source: @tass_agency/381540).
Ukrainian UAVs were observed heading toward Borzna in Chernihiv oblast, indicating operational focus on northern Ukrainian territories (Source: @kpszsu/65620).
The liberation of Yurkovka, 10 km from Sloviansk and Kramatorsk, may affect northern front dynamics by opening new Russian avenues of advance (Source: @tass_agency/381534).

Russian drone attacks on a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia caused eight injuries and significant material damage, underscoring intensified southern front aerial strikes (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
UAVs were reported heading toward Komyshuvakha in Zaporizhzhia oblast, suggesting continued Russian aerial pressure on southern supply routes (Source: @kpszsu/65631).
UAVs from the south were observed approaching Zaporizhzhia city, indicating ongoing hostile air activity in the southern theater (Source: @kpszsu/65606).
Ukrainian forces discussed a limited ceasefire zone of 50‑70 km, which would directly impact operations along the southern front lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31015).
Zelensky's ultimatum to Belarus to remove fire‑correction equipment along the border reflects concerns about southern flank security (Source: @UkraineNow/82059).
Two Ukrainian UAVs were shot down near Sevastopol, indicating active air defense engagements in the Black Sea region (Source: @tass_agency/381543).
Russian drones struck a logistics facility in Zaporizhzhia, causing eight injuries and a fire, highlighting continued UAV use by Russian forces (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Ukrainian UAVs conducted operations over Trubchevsk in Bryansk, injuring a child, showing cross‑border drone incursions (Source: @tass_agency/381512).

Multiple UAV sightings were reported across Dnipropetrovsk, Sumy, Chernihiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts, reflecting widespread aerial activity on both sides (Sources: @kpszsu/65605, @kpszsu/65620, @kpszsu/65631, @kpszsu/65606).
Ukrainian claims of a mass UAV attack on Moscow were announced, though details remain unverified (Source: @tass_agency/381516).

key_developments: Two Ukrainian UAVs were downed near Sevastopol, confirming active air defense operations in the Black Sea area (Source: @tass_agency/381543).
Russian drones hit a logistics building in Zaporizhzhia, injuring eight and causing a fire, indicating intensified aerial strikes on southern infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82058).
Russian forces reported destroying a surrounded enemy in Konstantinovka and advancing in the Nikolaevsky microdistrict, suggesting a localized offensive push (Source: @tass_agency/381540).
Civilian casualties rose as Russian attacks damaged two buses in Kherson, wounding five, and killed two in Kramatorsk with six more injured (Sources: @UkraineNow/82046, @UkraineNow/82053).
Ukrainian officials discussed a ceasefire concept limiting hostilities to a 50‑70 km zone on both sides of the front, potentially reshaping operational tempo (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31015).
Chemical grenades containing chloropicrin were discovered in Staromlynivka, raising concerns about chemical weapon deployment in liberated areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31030).
Zelensky warned Belarus to remove fire‑correction equipment within a week, threatening Ukrainian action if not complied (Source: @UkraineNow/82059).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial activity remains pervasive, with UAV strikes targeting logistics hubs in Zaporizhzhia and civilian transport in Kherson, while Ukrainian air defenses continue to intercept drones near Sevastopol. Ground offensives around Konstantinovka and Nikolaevsky indicate localized Russian attempts to exploit gaps after the liberation of Yurkovka. The discovery of chemical grenades in Staromlynivka adds a potential non‑conventional threat, complicating de‑mining and civilian safety in reclaimed territories.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities are bolstering air defense in the south and Black Sea regions, while diplomatic channels explore a limited ceasefire corridor to reduce front‑line pressure. Belarus faces heightened diplomatic tension after Zelensky's ultimatum, potentially influencing Belarusian border posturing. International observers are monitoring the chemical weapons allegation, which may prompt additional monitoring or sanctions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Continued UAV sightings across multiple oblasts suggest both sides are leveraging drone warfare for reconnaissance and strike missions, requiring updated counter‑UAV measures. The reported Russian advance in Nikolaevsky microdistrict warrants close surveillance for potential breakthrough attempts. The chemical grenade find underscores the need for hazardous material response teams in liberated zones. Diplomatic discussions on a 50‑70 km ceasefire zone could alter operational planning if formalized.
